exercises.title: SIGNAL NORMALIZE
exercises.brief: Clean a noisy transmission: lowercase, trim, collapse the gaps.
promptLines: Define **normalize(s)** that lower-cases the text, trims the ends, and collapses every run of internal whitespace down to a single space.
starterLines: // TODO: lowercase, trim, then squeeze runs of whitespace to one space

tests.name: lower and collapse
tests.code: assertEqual(normalize('  Hello   WORLD '), 'hello world', 'lowercase, trim, single spaces');

tests.name: multiple gaps
tests.code: assertEqual(normalize('JACK   IN'), 'jack in', 'internal run becomes one space');

tests.name: single word
tests.code: assertEqual(normalize('  Neo  '), 'neo', 'trim and lowercase one word');

exercises.lore: Raw feeds are full of jitter. Normalize before you parse.

exercises.title: LONGEST CALLSIGN
exercises.brief: Find the longest space-separated word in a string.
promptLines: Define **longestWord(s)** that returns the longest space-separated word. On a tie, return the **first** word that reaches the maximum length.
starterLines: // TODO: split on spaces, keep the longest (first wins ties)

tests.name: middle word
tests.code: assertEqual(longestWord('neo trinity tank'), 'trinity', 'trinity beats the rest');

tests.name: tie keeps first
tests.code: assertEqual(longestWord('a bb cc'), 'bb', 'first word at max length wins');

exercises.hint: Split on ' ', loop keeping a word only when length is strictly greater.
exercises.lore: On a crowded channel, the longest callsign cuts through.

exercises.title: PANGRAM PROBE
exercises.brief: Check whether a line uses every letter of the alphabet.
promptLines: Define **isPangram(s)** returning `true` only if the string contains every letter **a-z** at least once, case-insensitively.
isPangram('The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog')  // true
starterLines: // TODO: lowercase, then confirm a..z each appear at least once

tests.name: classic pangram
tests.code: assert(isPangram('The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog') === true, 'full alphabet is a pangram');

tests.name: missing letters
tests.code: assert(isPangram('hello world') === false, 'not every letter present');

exercises.hint: Lowercase, then loop char codes 97..122; if any indexOf is -1, return false.
exercises.lore: A clean keymap test: every glyph must fire at least once.

exercises.title: DOMINANT GLYPH
exercises.brief: Find the character that shows up the most, spaces excluded.
promptLines: Define **mostFrequentChar(s)** returning the character that appears most often, **ignoring spaces**. On a tie, return the character that **first** reaches the maximum count. Assume at least one non-space character.
starterLines: // TODO: tally each non-space char, track the first to hit the running max
solutionLines: if (counts[ch] > max) { max = counts[ch]; best = ch; }

tests.name: ignores spaces
tests.code: assertEqual(mostFrequentChar('a b a'), 'a', 'spaces are not counted');

exercises.hint: Skip ' ', count in an object, and update best only when the new count is strictly greater than max.
exercises.lore: Trace the traffic: one glyph always dominates the wire.

exercises.title: CAESAR CIPHER
exercises.brief: Shift the lowercase alphabet forward, wrapping past z.
promptLines: Define **caesar(s, n)** that shifts each lowercase letter a-z forward by `n` (wrapping z→a). Leave every other character unchanged. Assume `n` is 0..25.
starterLines: // TODO: shift only a-z within 0..25, wrap with % 26, keep the rest
exercises.hint: For codes 97..122 use (c - 97 + n) % 26 + 97; otherwise copy the char.
exercises.lore: Old crypto from a dead empire. Still fun to crack at runtime.
